Wait—I just noticed something crucial!
Is "**supporting cusps occlude**" actually referring to **tooth anatomy**?
Yes! In dentistry:
- "Supporting cusps" are often found on molars (e.g., mesiobuccal cusp supports disto-buccal).

- Occlusion happens at contact areas formed by these features.

→ So this is NOT an obstetric issue.
